title: Free-form dexe_graph_query against all three subgraphs (live mainnet)
notes: First live e2e for free-form dexe_graph_query (the unit test in tests/tools/graph-query.test.ts only covers the read-only guard). Read-only, zero gas. One bounded query per subgraph; step 1 also exercises GraphQL variables. Depends on The Graph gateway — a transient gateway failure is re-runnable.

args.query: query Top($n: Int!) { daoPools(first: $n, orderBy: votersCount, orderDirection: desc) { id name votersCount proposalCount } }
{ transactions(first: 5, orderBy: timestamp, orderDirection: desc) { id type user timestamp } }
{ validatorInPools(first: 5, orderBy: balance, orderDirection: desc) { id validatorAddress balance } }

successCriteria.check: pools.data.daoPools is a non-empty array; every row has id and name; votersCount is non-increasing across rows
interactions.data.transactions is a non-empty array; timestamps are descending
validators.data.validatorInPools is an array; rows (if any) have validatorAddress and balance
no step returned isError; every capture has {subgraph, data}
